questions.question: A learner says television and social media influence society in the same way because both spread messages widely. Which distinction gives the strongest sociological correction?
options: Television is more one-way, while social media allows greater user participation and feedback.
Television is a private medium, while social media is used only by formal institutions.
Both media have identical audience roles because users mainly receive fixed messages.
Social media has no link with mass communication because it involves individual devices.

questions.question: Statement I: Mass media can shape public opinion by selecting and framing issues. Statement II: Audiences interpret media messages only in the way producers intend. Which option is most accurate?
options: Statement I is correct, but Statement II ignores audience interpretation and social context.
Both statements are correct because audiences have no role in interpreting media content.
Statement I is incorrect because media only reflects society and does not shape opinion.
Statement II is correct because media effects are identical across all social groups.
